removed: The system shall assign responsibility for managing the budget to individual teams, not solely to the IFA.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget.
reason: Both assign budget management responsibility to teams; [2] is less specific than [0].

removed: The system shall allow the aggregated data to be accessible to all users.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow all users to view the complete aggregated data.
reason: Both state that aggregated data is accessible to all users; [13] is redundant with [9].

removed: The system shall they can only access their own data.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the insertion of budget transactions and items only by the respective teams.
reason: Both state that teams can only access their own data; [18] is less specific and redundant with [17].

removed: The system shall automatically generate the full match schedule for each league at the start of every season.
duplicate_of: The system shall automatically generate the season schedule for each league at the start of the season.
reason: Both describe automatic generation of the full match schedule at the start of the season; [100] duplicates [99].

removed: The system shall alert the IFA when a team's recorded salary expenditure exceeds the wage cap defined in the IFA budget policy.
duplicate_of: The system shall alert the IFA when a team's recorded salary expenditure exceeds the wage cap defined in the IFA budget policy.
reason: Both specify alerting the IFA when a team exceeds the wage cap; [110] is identical to [109].

removed: The system shall preserve the final league table and all match results for every completed season.
duplicate_of: The system shall preserve the final league table and all match results for every completed season permanently.
reason: Both require preserving final league tables and match results permanently; [119] duplicates [118].

removed: The system shall allow IFA administrators to generate a financial summary report for any specified date range.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow IFA administrators to generate a financial summary report for any specified date range.
reason: Both allow IFA administrators to generate financial summary reports for any date range; [123] duplicates [122].

removed: The system shall allow a fan to follow a specific football club so that they receive all notifications related to that club's matches and activities.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow fans to follow an entire football club to receive all notifications related to that club's matches and activities.
reason: Both describe fans following a club to receive related notifications; [116] is a rephrased duplicate of [115].

removed: The system shall when an IFA administrator modifies the generated schedule, the system shall require them to document their reason for the change and have a different IFA administrator authorise it.
duplicate_of: The system shall record the reason for any match schedule change made by an IFA administrator and require confirmation of the change by a second administrator.
reason: Both require documenting and authorizing schedule changes by IFA administrators; [103] adds no distinct behaviour beyond [102].

removed: The system shall implement reliable data backup mechanisms to prevent loss of financial and scheduling records.
duplicate_of: The system shall perform daily backups of all data.
reason: Both refer to data backup mechanisms; [88] is a general restatement of [87]'s daily backup requirement.

removed: The system shall require administrator accounts to use a second form of verification before granting access.
duplicate_of: The system shall require administrator accounts to use a second form of verification for accessing sensitive financial data.
reason: Both require second-factor verification for administrator access to sensitive data; [90] is less specific than [89].

removed: The system shall provide read-only guest access to users who have not registered.
duplicate_of: The system shall require users to have an account to perform any actions beyond read-only access, and shall grant read-only guest access to unregistered users.
reason: Both provide read-only guest access to unregistered users; [107] duplicates [105].

removed: The system shall be required to create an account and sign in to use the system.
duplicate_of: The system shall require users to have an account to perform any actions beyond read-only access, and shall grant read-only guest access to unregistered users.
reason: Both address user account requirements; [106] is a less complete version of [105]'s access rules.

removed: The system shall make all data accessible to everyone except for budget data, which shall be restricted.
duplicate_of: The system shall encrypt budget data to prevent unauthorized access or data leakage.
reason: Both address budget data restriction; [61] states access control while [62] specifies encryption as the method.

removed: The system shall alert administrators to budget violations.
duplicate_of: The system shall alert the IFA when a team's recorded salary expenditure exceeds the wage cap defined in the IFA budget policy.
reason: Both involve budget violation alerts, but [4] is generic while [109] specifies wage cap; kept separate due to scope difference.
